description: Student Debt in the United States: Panel A shows the total student debt outstanding in the U.S. between Q1 2006 and Q4 2021. Panel B plots the ratio of student debt outstanding to nominal GDP. The student debt and GDP data are from the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED). Panel C plots the number of people with student debt outstanding and Panel D reports the average outstanding balance per recipient. The data comes from the Office of Federal Student Aid (FSA).

Repayment choices under baseline and alternate plans: This figure shows the proportion of all households with student debt that choose to make payments according to the standard repayment plan (SRP), make payments under the income-driven repayment plan (IDRP), or skip the payment and become delinquent. Each bar represents one version of the model: baseline, Principal Payment Deferral (PPD), and Full Payment Deferral (FPD).

Consumption and saving over the lifecycle in alternate repayment plans: Panel A (B) shows the percentage difference between consumption (bond savings) in the modified contracts, Principal Payment Deferral (PPD) or Full Payment Deferral (FPD), relative to the baseline model for households in each group.
